stem: A school is teaching pupils how to choose strong passwords for their accounts. Five candidate guidelines are listed below.
model_answer: Strong passwords are long, mix character types and avoid personal information that an attacker could guess. Reusing the same password across sites makes a single breach unlock multiple accounts; writing the password on a sticky note moves the secret out of memory and into a place anyone in the room can read; choosing a pet's name picks a value an attacker can often find on social media.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Tick exactly two boxes that describe good practice when choosing a password.

options: Use a long passphrase mixing letters, numbers and symbols.
Use a different password for every account that holds important data.
Use the same password everywhere so it is easy to remember.
Write the password on a sticky note next to the computer.
Choose your pet's name because it is easy to remember.

mark_points.text: Use a long passphrase mixing letters, numbers and symbols.
Use a different password for every account that holds important data.
